alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services with experience scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs; demonstrates end-to-end ownership and integration across multiple teams.
alignments.job_requirement: Own the end-to-end EMI/EMC engineering for an airborne platform, including the control plan, compliance roadmap, and integration strategy across all subsystems.

alignments.candidate_evidence: Transformation program leadership with stakeholder management across global cross-functional teams.
alignments.job_requirement: Coordinate cross-functional design decisions with airframe, avionics, EW, comms, propulsion, power, and mission computing; manage EM trade-offs.

alignments.candidate_evidence: Experience managing transformation programs and cross-functional coordination suggests supplier/vendor engagement capability.
alignments.job_requirement: Manage supplier technical governance for EMI/EMC compliance and non-conformance closure.

gaps.gap: No electrical engineering, aerospace engineering, or related degree; candidate background is operations and business transformation.
gaps.mitigation: Would require substantial foundational technical ramp-up in electromagnetic theory, transmission lines, antenna design, and shielding principles—likely 12+ months of intensive study and mentorship.

gaps.gap: No hands-on EMI/EMC engineering experience or airborne platform systems work; no exposure to EM simulation tools (CST, FEKO, HFSS) or electromagnetic modeling.
gaps.mitigation: No practical mitigation; this is domain-specific technical expertise that cannot be quickly acquired through operations or strategy experience alone.

gaps.gap: No experience with military standards (MIL-STD-461, MIL-STD-464, DO-160, STANAG) or defense airworthiness certification frameworks.
gaps.mitigation: Standards knowledge is learnable but requires years of hands-on certification work in defense aerospace; not a substitute for baseline EM engineering capability.

gaps.gap: No demonstrated grounding, bonding, shielding, filtering, or cable segregation design experience; no lightning protection or composite structure analysis background.
gaps.mitigation: These are specialized subsystem-level technical skills that require deep electrical engineering knowledge; cannot be acquired through operations leadership alone.

hard_blockers.evidence: JD states: 'Hold a relevant degree in Electrical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or a related field' and 'Have proven experience in EMI/EMC engineering for airborne platforms with end-to-end ownership from requirements to certification.' These are explicitly positioned as required qualifications ('should apply if you'). Candidate has no electrical or aerospace engineering degree and zero EMI/EMC or airborne systems experience. The role is pure systems engineering requiring deep technical domain expertise in electromagnetic theory, simulation tools, and defense certification—fundamentally misaligned with a Strategy and Operations background.

output.summary: This is a specialist Systems Engineer role in electromagnetic interference for defense airborne platforms—a deep technical domain requiring an electrical or aerospace engineering degree, hands-on EMI/EMC certification experience, proficiency with EM modeling tools, and knowledge of military standards. The candidate is a Strategy and Operations leader with transformation and cross-functional leadership experience, but has no electrical engineering foundation, no EMI/EMC domain expertise, and no airborne systems background. The role is outside the target and approved-stretch families and presents hard blockers on both education and required technical experience.
uncertainties: Whether candidate has any hidden electrical engineering coursework or self-directed EM learning not captured in the profile.
